metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Students explore family dynamics, child development, relationship patterns, and communication strategies. This course examines how families function across diverse structures and cultures while developing interpersonal skills for healthy relationships.
metadata.general_description: An applied course exploring family dynamics, relationships, child development, and interpersonal communication.

big_ideas.statement: Healthy relationships are built on effective communication, mutual respect, and understanding of individual differences.
big_ideas.elaboration: Developing communication and listening skills enables deeper connections and resolution of conflicts in all relationships.

big_ideas.statement: Families take diverse forms and structures, each with unique strengths, challenges, and cultural values.
big_ideas.elaboration: Understanding family diversity helps students appreciate different approaches to family life while recognizing universal needs for belonging and support.

big_ideas.statement: Child development follows predictable patterns while being influenced by individual differences, environment, and relationships.
big_ideas.elaboration: Understanding developmental stages and individual variation enables more responsive and supportive interactions with children.

big_ideas.statement: Personal well-being and relationship health are supported by self-awareness, emotional regulation, and healthy boundaries.
big_ideas.elaboration: Developing self-awareness and interpersonal skills contributes to positive relationships and overall life satisfaction.

competencies.raw: Design programs and resources to support healthy relationships
i_can_statements: I can develop resources for relationship education
I can design communication workshops and activities
I can create conflict resolution frameworks
I can design programs supporting family well-being

competencies.raw: Plan approaches to support child development and family needs
i_can_statements: I can design age-appropriate activities for children
I can plan support systems for families at different life stages
I can develop educational resources for parenting
I can design inclusive approaches recognizing family diversity

competencies.raw: Develop strategies for managing relationships across life stages
i_can_statements: I can create strategies for navigating relationship changes
I can design approaches to building strong family relationships
I can develop plans for life transitions and challenges
I can create resources for relationship skill development

competencies.raw: Evaluate relationship quality and identify areas for growth
i_can_statements: I can assess relationship patterns and dynamics
I can identify communication strengths and areas for improvement
I can recognize unhealthy relationship patterns
I can recommend resources and support for relationships

competencies.raw: Demonstrate effective communication and listening skills
i_can_statements: I can practice active listening and empathetic responses
I can communicate thoughts and feelings clearly and respectfully
I can engage in discussions and disagree respectfully
I can adapt communication for different audiences and situations

competencies.raw: Apply conflict resolution and problem-solving skills
i_can_statements: I can identify sources of conflict in relationships
I can use negotiation and compromise strategies
I can apply problem-solving processes to relationship challenges
I can address conflict constructively and respectfully

competencies.raw: Demonstrate understanding of child development and appropriate interactions
i_can_statements: I can recognize developmental stages and characteristics
I can interact with children appropriately for their development
I can identify developmental concerns and respond appropriately
I can create safe and supportive environments for children

competencies.raw: Support healthy family relationships and dynamics
i_can_statements: I can contribute positively to family relationships
I can recognize and respect different family structures and values
I can support family members through transitions
I can advocate for family-centered practices

competencies.raw: Use communication technologies responsibly in relationships
i_can_statements: I can use digital communication tools effectively
I can maintain healthy boundaries in online interactions
I can recognize digital citizenship and etiquette
I can navigate digital communication challenges

competencies.raw: Access resources and information to support relationships
i_can_statements: I can research relationship and family resources
I can evaluate credibility of online information
I can access support services and community resources
I can use educational platforms to learn about relationships

competencies.raw: Use technology to create educational resources about relationships
i_can_statements: I can create presentations and resources about relationships
I can develop digital content for relationship education
I can use multimedia to communicate about family dynamics
I can share resources and information using technology

competencies.raw: Evaluate technology use and impacts on relationships
i_can_statements: I can assess impacts of technology on family relationships
I can identify healthy versus unhealthy technology use patterns
I can recognize online safety and privacy concerns
I can develop strategies for balanced technology use

areas.name: Family Structures and Diversity
topics: Diverse family structures: nuclear, extended, blended, single-parent, multi-generational
Cultural variations in family organization and values
Family dynamics and roles in different structures
Inclusivity and respect for family diversity
First Peoples family traditions and values
Family adaptation to changes and challenges

areas.name: Child Development and Care
topics: Developmental stages from infancy through adolescence
Physical, cognitive, social, and emotional development
Developmental milestones and variations
Attachment and bonding in early relationships
Play, learning, and developmental activities
Special needs and developmental differences

areas.name: Communication and Interpersonal Skills
topics: Effective communication patterns and skills
Active listening and empathetic responses
Nonverbal communication and body language
Managing emotions and emotional expression
Building trust and connection in relationships
Cultural differences in communication styles

areas.name: Relationships and Personal Well-being
topics: Relationship formation and development across the lifespan
Peer relationships, friendships, and romantic relationships
Conflict resolution and problem-solving in relationships
Boundaries and healthy relationship patterns
Recognizing unhealthy or abusive relationships
Supporting others through relationship challenges
